description_md:
As a compliance-officer, I want the Salesforce connector to automatically propagate record deletion events to the data platform so that deleted source records are properly handled according to retention policies and audit trails remain complete.

## Background

When records are deleted in Salesforce, the connector must detect and propagate these deletion events to maintain data consistency across the platform. This ensures compliance requirements are met and prevents orphaned PII data from persisting beyond its retention period.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. When a record is deleted in Salesforce, the connector detects the deletion within 5 minutes and marks the corresponding platform record as "deleted" without immediately removing it
2. The audit log captures the deletion event with timestamp, source record ID, tenant identifier, and the user who triggered the source deletion
3. Records marked as deleted are retained in the platform for the configured retention period (default 90 days) before permanent removal
4. A workspace-admin can view and filter deleted records in the Salesforce connector's data source view

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Investigating Salesforce deletion event detection mechanism. The connector health monitor will need to track these events separately from standard sync operations.
